scan_linter_check.description: The JSON.parse() function throws an exception in case the given parameter is not a valid JSON string. Throwing exceptions outside a module's scope is a bad practice and often leads to unhandled exceptions with unpredicatble results.
scan_linter_check.name: CodeSanity - Avoid JSON.parse()
scan_linter_check.resolution_details: The DevTools function ParseJson() encapsulates JSON.parse() and catches exceptions if thrown. In that case, the function returns false. Refactor the code to use ParseJson() instead of JSON.parse().
